articles.title: Anthropic suspends new AI tools over US government security concerns
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, released publicly this week, sparked concerns about cybersecurity and hacking.

articles.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
articles.summary: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans. The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) software kit called Outsider, per the tech giant. "The operation weaponized Gemini to help

articles.title: Israel carries out air strikes on Lebanon, state media says, as Iran claims deal with US near
articles.summary: The strikes come as Iran's foreign minister says a deal to end fighting with the US is close.

articles.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in airstrike, Trump says
articles.summary: Niño Guerrero was killed in a "swift and lethal kinetic strike," the US President wrote in a social media post.

articles.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
articles.summary: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the U.S., citing national security concerns. The AI company said it received an order at 5:21 p.m. ET, instructing it to suspend

articles.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
articles.summary: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. Sygnia, which tracks the group as Velvet Ant, says it backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components that decide who is allowed to sign in, planting its access where ordinary cleanup could not reach it. articles.title: Switzerland to vote on plan to cap population at 10 million
articles.summary: The right-wing Swiss People's Party calls the plan a "sustainability initiative", but opponents say it is a recipe for chaos.

articles.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire as SpaceX soars in stock market debut
articles.summary: Musk is now worth $1.11tn according to the Bloomberg rich list, while SpaceX listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange with a value of $2.2tn.

articles.title: Warner Bros $111bn sale to Paramount approved by US justice department
articles.summary: The approval marks a key development in the merger that will reshape media, allowing the continue of the takeover of the Hollywood studio, which owns CNN and HBO.

articles.title: French town buries murdered child as questions mount over police failings
articles.summary: The prime suspect in the killing of Lyhanna, 11, was reported to police nine months prior but never questioned.

articles.title: More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates
articles.summary: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is being pushed further into crisis as hostilities continue between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants – and essential services and healthcare are targeted.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe
articles.summary: Violence spread across Belfast following a shocking knife attack allegedly carried out by a Sudanese asylum seeker on Monday, triggering a wave of anti-immigration unrest. The victim suffered significant injuries to his face and back.

articles.title: NASA to Cover 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission Space Station Departure
articles.summary: NASA and its international partners are set to receive scientific research samples and hardware as a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft is scheduled to depart the International Space Station on Tuesday, June 16, for its return to Earth. Watch NASA’s live undocking coverage beginning at 11:45 a.m. EDT on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and the agency’s YouTube channel. […]

articles.title: NASA Award Boosts Space Technology Research Capabilities
articles.summary: NASA is introducing a new funding opportunity to accelerate academic research and technology development. The Minority University Research and Education Project Space Technology Artemis Research (M‑STAR) application window opened Thursday and will remain open through 11:59 p.m. EDT on Tuesday, Aug. 11. The research funded through this award supports the agency’s priorities for exploring the […]

articles.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have described what they say is a new class of attack that can trick artificial intelligence (AI) coding agents into running arbitrary code on developer machines. Called Agentjacking by Tenet Security, the attack can be triggered by means of a fake error report crafted using Sentry, an open-source error-tracking and performance-monitoring platform. articles.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
articles.summary: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else to handle the alert queue. MDR stepped in. It worked well enough. Until now. The threat landscape has changed faster than the MDR model can adapt. Attackers are using AI to move faster, generate more

articles.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution. LangGraph is an open-source framework created by LangChain to build complex, stateful, and multi-agent artificial intelligence (AI) agentic applications. articles.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
articles.summary: Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks. Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling of AudiA6 cut off a "key financial pipeline used to wash hundreds of millions in illicit profits." The service is estimated to have been used to launder more than €336 million (~$389 million) since the

articles.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
articles.summary: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs. Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and location pins that the agent executed without the victim ever seeing them. Varonis built a test agent on

articles.title: Fans on concourses instead of in seats, claims Fifa
articles.summary: Fifa says numerous empty seats were visible in the near-sell-out Group A match between South Korea and the Czech Republic because of fans remaining on concourses.

articles.title: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal
articles.summary: The United Nations continues to monitor developments amid conflicting reports on Friday of a possible ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran, the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General said in New York.

articles.title: ‘Perilous moment’ threatens to reverse years of gains in HIV/AIDS response
articles.summary: External funding cuts, a backlash against human rights, and chronic under-investment in HIV prevention and community services are threatening to reverse years of hard-won progress in the AIDS response, a UN report warned on Friday.

articles.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
articles.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection, and it is separate

articles.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
articles.summary: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender. articles.title: Married at First Sight Australia stars not told partners had drug and violence convictions
articles.summary: Stars of the hit TV show say they did not know their on-screen husbands had previous convictions.

articles.title: More time needed for deadly Air India crash inquiry, officials say
articles.summary: A statement says "significant progress" has been made into investigating the crash, in which 260 people died a year ago.

articles.title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
articles.summary: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

articles.title: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
articles.summary: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.

articles.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
articles.summary: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

articles.title: World News in Brief: State-supported safe houses in Haiti, EU pact strengthens refugee protection, demand for ‘critical minerals’ intensifies
articles.summary: Haiti has opened its first State-supported safe house for survivors of sexual violence with support from the UN, marking a significant step in responding to the growing crisis affecting women and girls.

articles.title: Hubble Sees Swarm of Galaxies
articles.summary: Looking somewhat like a swarm of bees returning to their hive, this NASA Hubble Space Telescope image features the galaxy cluster MACS0329-0211.

articles.title: NASA’s Chandra Discovers Possible Supernova Remnant in Galactic Center
articles.summary: Using data from NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory, astronomers may have found a supernova remnant in an intriguing neighborhood in the middle of our galaxy. A paper describing these new findings published in The Astrophysical Journal. articles.title: King leads tributes to 'giant of the art world' David Hockney
articles.summary: The artist, who has died at the age of 88, "was one of life's true originals", King Charles says.

articles.title: Ukraine civilian casualty toll in May highest in four years, UN monitors say
articles.summary: More civilians were killed and injured in Ukraine in May than in any other month in the past four years, UN investigators said on Friday in their latest update.

articles.title: WHO report shows progress in blood safety, but there are worrying gaps
articles.summary: Every day, safe blood helps save the lives of women experiencing childbirth complications, accident victims, cancer patients and people living with chronic diseases. Yet despite decades of progress, access to lifesaving blood remains deeply unequal, with shortages continuing to put lives at risk in many lower-income countries, according to a new World Health Organization (WHO) report.

articles.title: Hate speech spreading ‘faster than ever’, warns UN chief as global action plan is revealed
articles.summary: Hate speech is a grave and growing threat to peace and security, Secretary-General António Guterres warned on Thursday, calling for urgent action to break a cycle that can lead to genocide and other atrocity crimes.
